topic: Parallax = firmament wobble — c4 verification of c3 patch set (queue_id 302)
change_summary: c3 fingerprint: claim=27, finding=76, tldr_evidence=339, tldr_verdict=152, detail_evidence=1637, detail_verdict=1013. Current: claim=27, finding=76, tldr_evidence=298 (-41), tldr_verdict=215 (+63), detail_evidence=1637 (unchanged), detail_verdict=1259 (+246). Patches landed: (a) tldr_evidence 'regardless of brightness' -> 'regardless of distance' (closes c3 H1, also tightened ~40 chars of stylistic compression); (b) detail_verdict_text adds the '20 m wobble amplitude is not derived ... fitted backward from the textbook range' sentence (closes c3 H5); (c) detail_verdict_text replaces the old 'sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' boilerplate with 'there is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-017 appears only as a static entry in the dome WIN registry' (this is the cluster-A boilerplate sweep work being tracked under ISS-1841; closes the user-visible portion of c3 H2); (d) wins.json:WIN-017.code_analysis.monitoring flipped from 'hardcoded' to 'none' (closes the structural portion of c3 H2); (e) tldr_verdict expanded with 'The 0-0.5 arcsec parallax range is textbook physics; the 20 m wobble amplitude is reverse-engineered to match it' (closes c3 H6).
our_argument_summary: Same three-line rebuttal as c3: (1) Gaia DR3's 1.8 billion stars show 1/distance parallax which a single-amplitude rigid wobble cannot reproduce; (2) New Horizons' 7e9 km baseline measurement is geometrically impossible if the firmament is at 9,086 km; (3) the claim is structurally post-hoc — 0-0.5 arcsec is textbook physics and the 20 m amplitude is fitted to produce it via arctan(amp/H). Verdict 'Refuted by Data' continues to hold.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt L99 (only WIN-017 surface in current dome corpus)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No computation. WIN-017 exists only as a one-line CONFIRMED label in 02-wins.txt L99; no monitor.py / inject_ai_layer.py present in the cloned raw-text/ corpus; no derivation, no live fetch, no comparator. Same finding as c3 — confirmed unchanged this cycle.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — there is no check at all. The wins.json:WIN-017.code_analysis.monitoring tag is now correctly 'none' (was 'hardcoded' in c3, flipped by the c3 patch set).
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Static CONFIRMED label with no falsification criterion published. Any measured parallax can be retrofitted to a wobble-amplitude. Unchanged from c3.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: c3 flagged that the old 'hardcoded value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' wording referenced a fetch that doesn't exist. The c3 patch set replaced that wording with 'there is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-017 appears only as a static entry in the dome WIN registry — so even the nominal test that would check the hardcoded value against a live fetch is absent.' This now matches the corpus reality. No discrepancies remain at the user-visible layer.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Confirmed correct. wins.json:WIN-017.code_analysis.monitoring is now 'none' as of the c3 patch set. Per the strict rubric ('hardcoded' = monitor.py contains a static expected AND static observed), the absence of monitor.py and any parallax fetch in the cloned raw-text/ (verified this cycle: no monitor.py, no inject_ai_layer.py, no parallax/wobble entry in 03-predictions.txt, 05-model.txt, 08-live-power.txt, 07-ai-context.txt) means 'none' is the correct tag. The Part 2b monitoring breakdown should now reflect this — confirm via integrity scan.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c3. The dome model proposes a genuinely new mechanism (firmament lateral wobble) to explain parallax — mutually exclusive with standard orbital-baseline parallax. Standard physics has had a quantitative parallax mechanism since Bessel (1838); the dome substitutes a different mechanism rather than renaming it.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c3. Stellar parallax was first measured by Bessel (1838, 61 Cygni, 0.314 arcsec); the 0-0.5 arcsec range has been textbook physics since the late 19th century. The dome's 20 m amplitude is reverse-engineered: arctan(20/9,086,000) = 0.4540 arcsec, chosen to land in the known nearby-star parallax range. The new c3-patch sentence in detail_verdict_text now makes this explicit at the user-visible layer.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c3. The 20 m wobble amplitude is not a function of disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), sun_altitude (5,733 km), or any other dome parameter. It is the value required to produce arctan(amp/H) ≈ 0.454 arcsec at H=9,086 km. The c3-patch addition to detail_verdict_text now communicates this to the reader directly.

c3_H1_tldr_evidence_brightness_to_distan.verification: Current tldr_evidence reads: 'A wobbling firmament would produce the same parallax angle for all stars regardless of distance — the opposite of what's observed.' The brightness->distance swap landed cleanly. (Note: ISS-1848.last_verify_attempt_failure_reason='verification_pattern_failed' — that's a decider-side grep issue with em-dash encoding, not a content issue. The text itself contains the correct phrase per direct read of wins.json.)

c3_H2_monitoring_tag_to_none.verification: Two-part fix: (a) wins.json:WIN-017.code_analysis.monitoring flipped from 'hardcoded' to 'none' — verified directly. (b) The user-visible boilerplate in detail_verdict_text was rewritten from 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' to 'there is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-017 appears only as a static entry in the dome WIN registry — so even the nominal test that would check the hardcoded value against a live fetch is absent.' Both fixes are now consistent with the corpus reality. The cluster-A sweep (ISS-1841 covering WIN-017/018/019/029) is partially landed via this WIN-017 patch; the other three WINs are tracked under that issue.

c3_H3_cross_validation_circularity.verification: Current detail_evidence still contains the 'Cepheids, RR Lyrae, spectrophotometry — all methods agree' sentence without separating spectroscopic-distance independence. ISS-1847 (assigned to analyst, EXP-287) tracks the fix. Not a c3-patch-set hole; deferred per decider triage. Reaffirmed as the most likely defender objection if not addressed.

c3_H4_NH_projection_geometry.verification: Current detail_evidence still says NH 'measured parallax shifts of 32.4 arcsec (Proxima Centauri) and 15.7 arcsec (Wolf 359) that scale precisely with each star's distance' without the perpendicular-baseline qualifier. Severity remains minor (the published Throop 2020 figures match when projection is included; the 'scale precisely' claim is true at the geometry-projected level). Not blocking; can be addressed in a future polish pass alongside H3.

c3_H5_20m_amplitude_in_verdict_text.verification: Current detail_verdict_text contains: 'Nor is the 20 m wobble amplitude derived — it is the value required to produce arctan(amp/H) ≈ 0.454 arcsec at H=9,086 km, fitted backward from the textbook range.' This is exactly the c3 H5 recommendation. Closes the most predictable defender response to the post-hoc framing.

c3_H6_tldr_verdict_referent.verification: Current tldr_verdict reads: '1.8 billion Gaia measurements show distance-dependent parallax, impossible with a wobbling firmament. The 0-0.5 arcsec parallax range is textbook physics; the 20 m wobble amplitude is reverse-engineered to match it.' Both referents are now named (parallax range vs wobble amplitude). c3 H6 closed.

holes_found.description: POSSIBLE NEW HOLE — REVERSE-ENGINEERED RHETORIC. The c3-patch addition to detail_verdict_text uses 'fitted backward from the textbook range' and the new tldr_verdict says 'reverse-engineered to match it'. Both are accurate descriptions of derives_from_dome=false + post_hoc=true, but they are also the most rhetorically loaded phrasing in the WIN. A genuinely fair-minded dome defender could say: 'You don't know it was fitted; the dome model could have arrived at 20 m from independent considerations (e.g., the 11.79 Hz resonance) and the textbook match is corroborating, not circular.' We DO have grounds — derives_from_dome_evidence verifies no derivation path from dome parameters exists in the published material — but the rhetoric runs ahead of the structural finding. A small softening would lock the argument: 'fitted backward' -> 'matched to' (no change to the structural claim, removes the inference about authorial intent). Severity remains MINOR because absence of any published derivation in the dome corpus does justify the inference under a reasonable burden of proof.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish: change 'fitted backward from the textbook range' to 'matched to the textbook range' in detail_verdict_text. Same one-word swap in tldr_verdict ('reverse-engineered' -> 'calibrated'). Both edits remove the authorial-intent inference while preserving the falsifiability claim. NOT blocking — current text is defensible, just slightly more attackable than necessary.

holes_found.description: TLDR_EVIDENCE LENGTH SHRANK MORE THAN A SINGLE-WORD SWAP WOULD EXPLAIN. c3 baseline tldr_evidence_length=339; current=298 (delta -41 chars). The c3 H1 fix was a single-word brightness->distance swap (delta about -2 chars). The remaining ~39 chars of compression are uncategorized — likely a stylistic tightening that landed in the same edit window but isn't recorded in the queue item's reason field. Reading the current tldr_evidence: 'Gaia measured parallax for 1.8 billion stars. The pattern is unmistakable: parallax is inversely proportional to distance, exactly as expected for an orbital baseline. A wobbling firmament would produce the same parallax angle for all stars regardless of distance — the opposite of what's observed.' This reads cleanly and the argument is preserved; no factual loss visible. But for audit-trail integrity, an undocumented edit landed alongside the documented one. Severity MINOR (no content harm, audit-trail bookkeeping only).
holes_found.recommendation: Note in the changelog (or human-note) that the c3 H1 patch also tightened the surrounding TLDR sentence. No content rollback needed.

stronger_arguments.description: Carry-forward from c3 (still applicable): binary-pair parallax co-equality. Gravitationally bound binaries like Alpha Centauri A/B have essentially identical Gaia parallaxes (within milliarcseconds), but field stars at the same angular position have different parallaxes if at different distances. Under firmament wobble, all stars in a small angular patch should show the same wobble-induced shift regardless of physical association. This is a separate independent line from New Horizons.
sources: Gaia DR3 catalog; Kervella et al. 2021 A&A on Alpha Cen astrometry.

stronger_arguments.description: Carry-forward from c3 (still applicable): Hipparcos-Gaia parallax agreement across two independent space missions, 30 years apart, with completely different optical chains. If parallax were a firmament-wobble artifact, the wobble would have to remain stationary in amplitude and phase across 30 years and across both spacecraft's orbital geometries — an unfalsifiable coincidence.

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c3 (data/wins.json:WIN-017 detail_evidence reaffirmed this cycle). The dome model correctly identifies that stellar parallax is small (sub-arcsecond for nearby stars and undetectable until 1838) and that 0-0.5 arcsec is the right ballpark for the nearby population. It also implicitly notes that a rigid lateral displacement of an observation surface produces an apparent shift via arctan — the geometry is not nonsense. The 20 m at 9,086 km arithmetic genuinely lands in the right magnitude range, which is more than can be said for many WINs that fail dimensionally (cf. data/wins.json:WIN-029 Schumann 5.3% off, data/wins.json:WIN-067 Antarctic-gravity 1,430x off). This kernel is real and we keep acknowledging it.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three independent failure modes, all reaffirmed this cycle: (a) the dome's mechanism is a single-amplitude rigid wobble that cannot reproduce the 1/d pattern across 1.8B Gaia stars (data/wins.json:WIN-017 detail_evidence paragraph 1, unchanged); (b) the New Horizons 7e9 km baseline measurement is geometrically impossible if the firmament is at 9,086 km (data/wins.json:WIN-017 detail_evidence paragraph 3; NH/firmament ratio = 770,432x reverified this review); (c) the 20 m amplitude is not derived from dome geometry — fitted backward to match the textbook range (now stated explicitly in data/wins.json:WIN-017 detail_verdict_text via the c3 H5 patch). Closing any one of the three does not save the claim.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender, given the c3-patched text, has fewer footholds than they did pre-patch. The two surviving moves: (i) the cross-validation circularity argument against 'Cepheids/RR Lyrae/spectrophotometry — all methods agree' (the period-luminosity calibration is parallax-anchored at the bottom rung of the distance ladder, so 'all methods agree' is partially circular); ISS-1847/EXP-287 tracks the preemption fix. (ii) the rhetoric attack on 'reverse-engineered' / 'fitted backward' wording — defender concedes derives_from_dome=false but objects that we don't know the dome author's process, only the absence of published derivation; this is a fair narrow point about authorial-intent inference. The 'New Horizons is still under the dome edge' move and the 'wobble calibrated to 11.79 Hz resonance' move both still collapse against existing text (disc_radius=20,015 km < 7e9 km by 350,000x; no published 11.79->20m derivation).
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Keep ISS-1847/EXP-287 on the analyst queue (highest-leverage open hole). Optional rhetoric softening from holes_found h1 above is low-priority. The c3 patch set materially shrunk the defender attack surface from 3 surviving moves (c3 advocate_mode.defense_survives=3) to 2.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Same as c3 — no cross-WIN inconsistencies introduced by the c3 patch set. WIN-016 (annual aberration, Refuted by Data) and WIN-017 both rely on Gaia astrometric data; WIN-016 argues against a refractive aberration mechanism while WIN-017 argues against firmament-wobble parallax — internally consistent (we trust Gaia as a precision astrometric instrument in both cases). WIN-029 (Schumann) uses 9,572 km from c/(4f) vs 9,086 km from dome cosmology; WIN-017 uses the cosmology value 9,086 km charitably. WIN-026 (crepuscular rays) uses sun_altitude 5,733 km — does not interact with parallax. Cross-WIN consistency reaffirmed (data/wins.json:WIN-016 detail_evidence; data/wins.json:WIN-029 detail_evidence; data/wins.json:WIN-026 detail_evidence).

claims_checked: 20 m wobble at H=9,086 km gives ~0.454 arcsec
Proxima/Sirius distance ratio 2x and parallax ratio 2x
New Horizons 7e9 km vs firmament 9,086 km = 770,000x ratio
tldr_evidence_length and detail_verdict_length deltas match the patch set
quantitative_verification.computations: Re-verified this cycle: arctan(20/9,086,000) * 180/pi * 3600 = 0.4540 arcsec (matches text). 8.6/4.24 = 2.028 (Sirius/Proxima distance); 0.768/0.379 = 2.027 (parallax ratio). 7e9/9,086 = 770,432 (matches '770,000x'). Patch fingerprint deltas: tldr_evidence -41 chars, tldr_verdict +63 chars, detail_verdict +246 chars, detail_evidence unchanged — consistent with the documented patch set (modulo the un-documented stylistic tightening of tldr_evidence flagged in holes_found h2).
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: None at the load-bearing argument level. The c3 H4 (NH projection geometry) minor remains uncovered but is not blocking and is not part of the c3 patch set.

deeper_analysis:
c4 verification pass for queue_id 302. The c3 patch set landed cleanly: 4 of the 6 c3 holes are CLOSED in the current wins.json (H1 brightness->distance, H2 monitoring tag + boilerplate, H5 20m amplitude in verdict text, H6 tldr_verdict referent). The 2 remaining c3 holes are tracked elsewhere or deferred: H3 (cross-validation circularity) is assigned to analyst as ISS-1847/EXP-287; H4 (NH projection geometry, minor) remains untracked but not blocking. Verdict 'Refuted by Data' continues to hold with high confidence — the three independent failure modes reaffirmed this cycle (1/d pattern across 1.8B Gaia stars; New Horizons 770,000x geometry; 20m amplitude not derived from dome parameters).

Two new minor holes flagged this cycle: (h1) the 'fitted backward' / 'reverse-engineered' rhetoric runs slightly ahead of the structural finding (we know derives_from_dome=false; we don't know the author's intent). One-word softening recommended but optional. (h2) tldr_evidence shrank 41 chars during the c3 H1 patch; only ~2 of those chars are accounted for by the brightness->distance swap. The remaining stylistic compression is undocumented in the queue reason field. Audit-trail bookkeeping issue, no content harm.

Advocate-mode pass: the patches reduced the surviving defense moves from 3 (c3) to 2 (c4) — cross-validation circularity (tracked under EXP-287) and rhetoric narrow attack on authorial-intent inference. Both are minor and one is already in flight.

Cross-WIN consistency check passes against WIN-016, WIN-029, WIN-026 — no contradictions introduced by the patches. Quantitative verification reproduces all four arithmetic claims and the fingerprint deltas match the documented patch set (modulo h2's undocumented tightening).

Confidence 0.92 (up from c3's 0.88) — the c3 patch set materially closed the largest attack surface (the TLDR factual error H1 was the most damaging because TLDRs are what readers see first). The remaining gaps are tracked or low-severity.

Recommended_action no_change: no further patch work needed for queue_id 302. The two new minor holes are optional-polish severity; let them ride to a future spot-check or fold them into the EXP-287 prose pass when it lands.

citations_verified: Gaia DR3 (Gaia Collaboration et al. 2023, A&A 674, A1) DOI 10.1051/0004-6361/202243940 — DOI format valid (carry-forward from c3).
Gaia DR3 archive page at cosmos.esa.int/web/gaia/dr3 — correct ESA archive URL (carry-forward).
NASA New Horizons interstellar parallax press page at nasa.gov — URL format valid (carry-forward).
20 m -> 0.4540 arcsec arithmetic — re-verified this cycle.
7e9 km / 9,086 km = 770,432 ratio — re-verified this cycle.
citations_unchecked: Throop et al. 2020 Research Notes AAS 4, 74 — still unchecked; carry-forward from c3. Citation format consistent with the journal's volume/article-number convention; the 32.4/15.7 arcsec figures match geometry-projected values for the published baseline (per c3 quantitative_verification). Verifying against the published article remains a future-cycle task.
